questions.question: Briefly explain the principle of 'Availability' in the context of the CIA Triad and provide one example of a threat to it.
expectedPoints: Definition: Availability ensures systems and data are accessible and operational when needed by authorized users.
Threat Example: A Denial-of-Service (DoS) attack that floods a web server with traffic, making it unavailable to legitimate customers.

questions.question: Describe the Defense in Depth strategy. Explain why it is effective and illustrate your answer by describing at least four distinct layers of control used to protect a corporate database server.
expectedPoints: Definition: A security strategy that employs multiple, layered, and diverse controls to protect an asset.
Why Effective: It eliminates single points of failure; increases an attacker's work factor, cost, and time; provides redundancy.
Layer 1 Example: Physical security (locked server room) to prevent physical access.
Layer 2 Example: Network security (firewall) to filter incoming/outgoing traffic.
Layer 3 Example: Endpoint security (host-based IPS) on the server itself to detect malicious activity.
Layer 4 Example: Data security (encryption) on the database files to protect confidentiality if other layers are breached.
